description: To bind oneself to a course of action; to pledge with intention to follow through. 'Seru' names the moment of decision—when possibility becomes promise.
sound_symbolism: The word seals with purpose. 'Se' sets with firm intention, decision crystallizing. 'Ru' runs with committed follow-through, action flowing from pledge.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi seru' (I commit). Takes object of commitment. Related to 'wenai' (dedicate) but emphasizes the binding moment over ongoing devotion.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Commitment as vow—the intention that shapes practice, renewed moment by moment.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Committed dialogue—the pledge to remain in conversation even when difficult.
